# opnsense_openvpn_generate_key (Ephemeral Resource)

Generates a fresh OpenVPN static key by calling `/openvpn/instances/gen_key` on each apply. The generated `key` is exposed only in-memory during the run — it is never written to Terraform state. Ephemeral values can be consumed by provider configurations, by other ephemeral blocks, and by `ephemeral = true` outputs; they cannot be passed to a regular resource argument (use a write-only attribute or a secrets-manager provider instead).

## Example Usage

```terraform
// Generate a fresh tls-crypt key on every apply.
// The result is exposed only at apply time and is never written to Terraform
// state.
ephemeral "opnsense_openvpn_generate_key" "tls_crypt" {
  key_type = "tls-crypt"
}

// Ephemeral values can only be consumed by other ephemeral blocks, by
// provider configuration, or by `ephemeral = true` outputs / write-only
// attributes — never by a regular resource argument. The output below is
// the typical pattern when piping the key into a downstream provider such
// as a secrets manager.
output "tls_crypt_key" {
  value     = ephemeral.opnsense_openvpn_generate_key.tls_crypt.key
  ephemeral = true
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `key_type` (String) Which key flavour to generate. One of `secret` (default, plain shared secret), `tls-auth`, `tls-crypt`, `tls-crypt-v2-server`, or `tls-crypt-v2-client`. Defaults to `secret` when unset.

### Read-Only

- `key` (String, Sensitive) The freshly generated key material in OpenVPN static-key format.
